fields.name: Startup Best Practices
fields.description: Essential engineering best practices for startup and small team projects. Covers documentation, security files, development hygiene, and CI/CD configuration.

fields.name: README.md exists
fields.description: Every repository must have a README.md file that documents the project purpose, setup instructions, and usage.
fields.remediation_hint: Create a README.md file in the repository root with project description, installation steps, and usage examples.

fields.name: LICENSE file exists
fields.description: Repository must have a LICENSE file specifying the terms under which the code can be used.
fields.remediation_hint: Add a LICENSE file to the repository root. Common choices: MIT, Apache 2.0, or GPL for open source projects.

fields.name: SECURITY.md exists
fields.description: Repository should have a SECURITY.md file explaining how to report security vulnerabilities.
fields.remediation_hint: Create a SECURITY.md file with instructions for responsible disclosure, including contact email and expected response time.

fields.name: CONTRIBUTING.md exists
fields.description: Repository should have contribution guidelines for new contributors.
fields.remediation_hint: Add a CONTRIBUTING.md file with guidelines for how to contribute, coding standards, and pull request process.

fields.name: No .env file committed
fields.description: Environment files containing secrets should never be committed to the repository.
fields.remediation_hint: Remove .env from the repository using 'git rm --cached .env' and add it to .gitignore. Never commit secrets!

fields.name: .gitignore exists
fields.description: Repository must have a .gitignore file to prevent accidental commits of unwanted files.
fields.remediation_hint: Create a .gitignore file appropriate for your project type. Include common patterns like node_modules/, *.pyc, .env, etc.

fields.name: GitHub Actions configured
fields.description: Repository should have CI/CD configured using GitHub Actions.
fields.remediation_hint: Create a .github/workflows directory with at least one workflow file (e.g., ci.yml) for automated testing.

fields.name: Tests directory exists
fields.description: Repository should have a dedicated tests directory for organized test files.
fields.remediation_hint: Create a tests/ directory and add unit tests for your code. Consider using pytest (Python), Jest (JavaScript), or your language's standard test framework.

fields.name: No secrets in repository
fields.description: Check for common secret patterns that should not be committed.
fields.remediation_hint: Remove any secret files from the repository and add them to .gitignore. Use environment variables or secret management tools instead.

fields.name: .env.example template exists
fields.description: Repository should have an .env.example file documenting required environment variables.
fields.remediation_hint: Create an .env.example file listing all required environment variables with placeholder values or descriptions.
